assertion_results.text: The answer recommends `monkeypatch` for controlling environment variables
assertion_results.evidence: Mentions "monkeypatch for environment" and specifically "monkeypatch.setenv("APP_MODE", "test") to control the env var" and "monkeypatch.delenv("APP_MODE", raising=False)".

assertion_results.text: The answer recommends `tmp_path` or an equivalent pytest temporary path fixture for isolated filesystem writes
assertion_results.evidence: Explicitly states "tmp_path for an isolated, auto-cleaned temporary directory to hold the JSON outputs" and uses tmp_path in the example test and with monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path).

assertion_results.text: The answer keeps the strategy narrow and explicitly avoids relying on real environment state or repository files
assertion_results.evidence: Uses monkeypatch to set/del APP_MODE (avoids real env) and "monkeypatch.chdir(tmp_path) to force all relative writes into a temp dir" with a code comment "# keep repo clean".
